classification: CONFIDENTIAL — Board / CEO / CRO / CISO / CAIO / Chief Architect / Head of AI Research / Head of AI Platform Engineering / Head of MRM / Head of Internal Audit / GC / DPO / AI Safety Lead / Treaty Liaison / PMO / Engineering Leadership / External Auditors / Supervisor Liaison
title: Institutional AGI/ASI & Enterprise AI Governance — Master Reference 2026-2030
subtitle: Comprehensive, implementation-focused master reference for Fortune 500, Global 2000 and G-SIFI institutions covering governance pillars, regulatory alignment (EU AI Act, NIST AI RMF 1.0, ISO/IEC 42001, OECD, GDPR, FCRA/ECOA, Basel III, SR 11-7, PRA, FCA, MAS, HKMA, SMCR, Consumer Duty, EO 14110), enterprise reference architectures (Kafka WORM, Docker Swarm, OPA, Terraform/CI/CD), FinServ MRM, AGI/ASI safety & containment (Sentinel v2.4, WorkflowAI Pro, Luminous Engine Codex, Cognitive Resonance, MGAS), global compute governance (ICGC, GACP, GACRA, GASO, GFMCF, GAICS, GAIVS, GATI, GACMO, FTEWS, GAI-SOC, GAIGA, GACRLS, GFCO, GAID, GASCF), Enterprise AI Governance Hub + AI Safety Report Generator, prompt engineering, civilizational corpus, regulator-ready report sections with <title>/<abstract>/<content> tags, and implementation blueprints with machine-readable artifacts
owner: Chief Architect + CAIO + AI Safety Lead + Head of AI Platform Engineering; co-signed by CRO, CISO, Head of MRM, GC, DPO, Treaty Liaison, PMO Director, Board AI/Risk Committee Chair
regimes: FCRA + ECOA + Reg B + Reg Z

directive.format: machine-parsable XML-style block consumed by Annex IV / SR 11-7 generators, AI Safety Report Generator, supervisor pack assembler, civilizational corpus indexer, and Enterprise AI Governance Hub federation

modules.title: M1 — Nine Governance Pillars
modules.summary: Nine pillars of institutional AGI/ASI + Enterprise AI governance that map onto every regulatory regime and operational control: Accountability, Transparency & Explainability, Fairness & Non-Discrimination, Privacy & Data Protection, Security & Resilience, Safety & Containment, Model Risk Management, Human Oversight, Sustainability & Societal Impact.

content.P1-Accountability: SMCR senior-manager mapping, RACI register, board AI charter, signed sign-offs anchored in WORM
content.P2-Transparency: Annex IV technical file, public model cards, decision-rationale logs (CRS-UUID linked), supervisor self-serve portal
content.evidence: SMCR map + ML-DSA signed sign-off ledger + Annex IV + model-card portal + decision logs

content.P3-Fairness: Demographic parity, equalized odds, calibration tests, FCRA/ECOA disparate-impact reviews, Reg B notices
content.P4-Privacy: GDPR DPIA, Arts 22 + 25 + 32 + 35, opt-out cascade, FPE tokenization, PETs (Opacus DP, SEV-SNP/TDX, BYOK)
content.evidence: Fairness eval matrix + adverse-action notice library + DPIA + opt-out lineage

content.P5-Security: Sigstore + SLSA L3+ + PQC (ML-DSA-65 + ML-KEM-768) + FIPS 140-3 L4 HSM + zero-egress K8s + WORM
content.P6-Safety: Sentinel v2.4 Cognitive Resonance, kill-switch quorum, MGAS containment, frontier-eval cluster
content.evidence: Signed SBOM + provenance + kill-switch SLA log + Sentinel evidence + containment proof

content.P7-MRM: SR 11-7 + OCC 2011-12 conceptual soundness, ongoing monitoring, independent validation, model inventory
content.P8-Oversight: Three-of-five kill-switch quorum, human-in-the-loop for Tier-1, board approval gates, SMCR personal accountability
content.evidence: MRM file per model + validation memos + override ledger + quorum approvals

content.P9-Sustainability: Compute carbon ledger, water-use accounting, OECD/UNESCO societal-impact assessments
content.metrics: kgCO2e per inference + per training run; water L per MWh; societal-impact score per use-case

modules.title: M2 — Regulatory Alignment Matrix (EU/UK/US/APAC + Global)
modules.summary: Cross-regime alignment of governance controls covering EU AI Act + Annex IV, NIST AI RMF 1.0 + GAI Profile, ISO/IEC 42001 + 23894 + 5338 + 38507, OECD AI Principles 2024, GDPR, FCRA/ECOA + Reg B/Z, Basel III/IV + BCBS 239/261, SR 11-7 + OCC 2011-12 + SR 15-18, PRA SS1/23 + SMCR + Solvency II, FCA Consumer Duty + PRIN 2A + SYSC, MAS FEAT + AI Verify + TRMG, HKMA GL-90, DORA + NIS2, US EO 14110 + OMB M-24-10.

content.EU AI Act 2026: Risk classes (Prohibited / High / Limited / Minimal); Annex III high-risk list; Annex IV technical file; CE marking; post-market monitoring; serious-incident reporting
content.UK GDPR + DPA 2018: ICO codes + AI guidance + UK PETs sandbox
content.PRA SS1/23: Model risk management principles + governance + validation + ongoing monitoring
content.SMCR: Senior-manager mapping + statements of responsibility + conduct rules
content.FCA Consumer Duty + PRIN 2A: Cross-cutting outcomes: products/services, price/value, consumer understanding, consumer support

content.EO 14110 + OMB M-24-10: Federal AI use-case inventory + impact assessments + safety testing + reporting to OMB
content.SR 11-7 + OCC 2011-12: Model definition + lifecycle + validation + governance + documentation
content.SR 15-18: Operational risk capital + AMA principles + stress testing
content.FCRA + ECOA + Reg B: Adverse-action notices + fair-lending tests + disparate-impact remediation

content.MAS FEAT + TRMG: Fairness, Ethics, Accountability, Transparency principles + Technology Risk Management Guidelines
content.AI Verify (IMDA): Self-assessment framework + technical tests + process checklists
content.HKMA GL-90: Genuine AI in HK banking + governance + risk management

content.OECD AI Principles 2024: 5 principles: inclusive growth, human-centred, transparency, robustness, accountability
content.G7 Hiroshima Process: International code of conduct + reporting framework
content.Bletchley + Seoul Declarations: Frontier safety + capability evaluations + responsible scaling policies
content.Council of Europe AI Convention: Binding international treaty (HR + democracy + rule of law)

content.Pharma: FDA GMLP + EMA reflection paper on AI + ICH Q9
content.Insurance: NAIC AI Model Bulletin + Solvency II
content.CriticalInfra: NERC CIP + EU NIS2 + sector ISACs

modules.title: M3 — Enterprise AI Reference Architectures
modules.summary: Reference architectures combining Kafka-based WORM audit logging, Docker Swarm + Kubernetes security, governance sidecars, explainability frontends, OPA-based compliance-as-code, Terraform/CI/CD governance automation, hyperparameter control standards, PQC KMS and Sentinel v2.4 hooks.

content.topology: MSK 3-broker quorum + S3 Object Lock Compliance + cross-region replication
content.retention: 7 yr baseline; 25 yr Annex IV high-risk; 100 yr civilizational sims
content.anchoring: Daily Merkle root publish + ML-DSA-65 + Sigstore + public verifier endpoint
content.ACL: Kafka ACL (read/write/admin/idempotent) per principal + per topic; managed via OPA

content.swarm: Encrypted overlay network + Raft logs encrypted; secrets via Vault + PQC envelope
content.k8s: Gatekeeper + Kyverno + OPA sidecar; Cilium L7 zero-egress; Kata Confidential runtime on Tier-1
content.admission: Sigstore cosign keyless OIDC + SLSA L3+ + ML-DSA hybrid co-signature
content.runtime: AppArmor + Seccomp + read-only rootfs + non-root UID + read-only volumeMounts

content.sidecar-opa: Envoy + OPA sidecar; policy bundle service signed + verified at load
content.sidecar-sentinel: Cognitive Resonance probes (Δ_drift ≤ 4%, latent ≤ 3%, fiduciary cosine ≥ 0.92, judge κ ≥ 0.9)
content.explainability-fe: SHAP/LIME plots + counterfactual explorer + decision-rationale viewer; supervisor-grade access controls

content.opa: Rego policy library: admission, egress, model-registry-required, prompt-approval, eval-pass, kill-switch quorum
content.bundle: Signed Rego bundles (ML-DSA-65); bundle service mirrored air-gapped
content.terraform: Modules: vpc, eks, msk, s3-worm, kms-pqc, iam, opa-bundle-svc, sigstore-mirror, sentinel-cluster
content.ci-cd: GitHub Actions reusable workflow: build → sign → attest → policy-gate (conftest + Gatekeeper) → deploy
content.gate-evidence: Each CI run emits signed evidence pack + Rekor entry + Merkle anchor

content.registry: Model manifest declares allowed hyperparameter ranges; deviations require MRM + GC approval
content.telemetry: Drift on hyperparam choice over time tracked + alerted
content.validation: Pre-prod runs across hp grid; canary deploys with rollback on κ < 0.9
content.audit: Hyperparam choices written to WORM with CRS-UUID lineage
content.freeze: Tier-1 hyperparam changes freeze 5 days before phase gate

modules.title: M4 — Financial Services Model Risk Management (SR 11-7, PRA SS1/23, BCBS 239)
modules.summary: FinServ-specific MRM lifecycle aligned to SR 11-7, OCC 2011-12, SR 15-18, PRA SS1/23, BCBS 239/261, Basel III/IV; covers model inventory, conceptual soundness, validation, ongoing monitoring, capital impact and disclosure.

content.definition: Model = quantitative method producing output for business decision; AI/ML in scope
content.metadata: Owner, validator, last review, regulator notification status, fairness flag
content.tools: Model Registry (WP-051 M11) + Annex IV bindings + SR 11-7 fields

content.checks: Mathematical correctness, data quality, assumptions, limitations, alternative methods reviewed
content.documentation: Model card + technical file + Annex IV section bindings + adverse-action notice draft
content.review: Independent reviewer NOT on dev team; sign-off into WORM

content.scope: Conceptual soundness, ongoing monitoring effectiveness, outcomes analysis, benchmarking
content.validators: Independent MRM team with reporting line outside the business

content.metrics: PSI, KS, Gini, AUC, calibration drift, fairness drift, business KPI alignment
content.thresholds: Per-model SLAs with auto-alert and auto-rollback on breach
content.cadence: Real-time + daily + weekly + monthly + quarterly review packs
content.wormEvidence: All monitoring outputs anchored to WORM with Merkle proof

content.Basel III/IV: AI models feeding IRB or VaR require regulator approval + ongoing monitoring; AMA op-risk capital
content.SR 15-18: Operational-risk capital for AI/automation failures + stress testing
content.FCA Consumer Duty: Outcome-based fairness; foreseeable harm assessment; vulnerable customer overlay
content.FCRA/ECOA: Adverse-action notices, fair-lending tests, disparate-impact remediation; Reg B notices
content.publicDisclosure: Pillar 3 + ESG + AI use-case inventory for federal agencies (US)

modules.title: M5 — AGI/ASI Safety & Containment Frameworks
modules.summary: AGI/ASI safety & containment: Sentinel v2.4 platform, WorkflowAI Pro, Luminous Engine Codex, Cognitive Resonance Protocol, crisis simulations, Minimum Viable AGI Governance Stack (MVAGS) + Minimum Governance Kernel (MGK), containment tiers T0..T4.

content.components: Cognitive Resonance probes; deception detector; mech-interp library; eval gating; kill-switch fabric
content.metrics: Δ_drift ≤ 4 %, latent Δ ≤ 3 %, fiduciary cosine ≥ 0.92, judge κ ≥ 0.9
content.deployment: Pre-prod gate + prod runtime probes + offline frontier evals
content.integration: OPA admission + WORM evidence + Annex IV + SR 11-7 packs

content.scope: Agent registry, CRS-UUID lineage, capability cards, agent-level OPA policies
content.controls: Tool-use allow-list, plan/act/critique split, evidence emission, human-in-loop for Tier-1
content.telemetry: Plan-vs-execute divergence, tool-call drift, fiduciary cosine, judge κ
content.kill-switch: Per-agent + global; 3-of-5 quorum; ≤ 60 s logical SLA

sections.title: Luminous Engine Codex + Cognitive Resonance Protocol (CRP)

content.codex: Reference taxonomy of governance primitives: agent, policy, prompt, model, eval, evidence, anchor
content.CRP: Resonance metric set linking representation similarity, behavioural alignment, and judge agreement
content.thresholds: CRP composite ≥ 0.9 for Tier-1 deploy; ≥ 0.95 for high-risk Annex IV
content.evidence: CRP run logs anchored daily; supervisor-grade replay (diff = 0)

content.cadence: Quarterly internal + annual AISI joint; outcomes anchored + remediation tracked

sections.title: Minimum Viable AGI Governance Stack (MVAGS) + MGK + Containment Tiers

content.MVAGS: OPA + Sigstore + WORM + PQC + Kill-switch + Sentinel + Registry + Eval — required to scale beyond Tier-2
content.MGK: Minimum Governance Kernel — invariants (audit, kill, registry, eval, policy) machine-checkable
content.T1: Pre-prod — synthetic + masked data; full Sentinel; canary
content.T2: Prod limited — production data; OPA + WORM + kill-switch active
content.T3: Prod full — Tier-1 customer-facing; SR 11-7 + Annex IV evidence packs continuous
content.T4: Frontier — air-gapped + 3-of-5 quorum required for any run + AISI co-supervision

modules.title: M6 — Global AI & Compute Governance Proposals (ICGC + 16 bodies)
modules.summary: International Compute Governance Consortium (ICGC) + global compute registries + treaty-aligned systemic-risk governance with sixteen proposed bodies: GACRA, GASO, GFMCF, GAICS, GAIVS, GACP, GATI, GACMO, FTEWS, GAI-SOC, GAIGA, GACRLS, GFCO, GAID, GASCF and umbrella GAI-COORD.

content.mission: Multilateral consortium overseeing frontier-scale compute access and audit
content.instruments: Compute registry, compute quotas, sanctions-aligned export controls, audit reciprocity
content.membership: G7 + G20 + invited middle powers + multi-stakeholder observers
content.secretariat: Rotating chair; permanent technical secretariat; AISI-aligned
content.evidence: Registry attestations, compute receipts, quota balance, public dashboard

content.GACRA: Global AI Compliance & Risk Authority — treaty-level standards harmonization
content.GASO: Global AI Safety Organisation — frontier-eval coordination + reporting framework
content.GFMCF: Global Frontier-Model Capability Framework — shared capability scale + thresholds
content.GAICS: Global AI Critical-incidents System — mandatory reporting + cross-border response
content.GAIVS: Global AI Verification System — independent verification + assurance for treaty obligations
content.GACP: Global AI Compute Passport — per-entity compute attestation, cross-border recognition
content.GATI: Global AI Threat-Intelligence — shared TIP across nations + ISACs
content.GACMO: Global AI Compute Market Oversight — anti-abuse + dominant-position monitoring

content.FTEWS: Frontier-Threat Early-Warning System — joint sensor network + sims + AISI co-op
content.GAI-SOC: Global AI Security Operations Centre — 24/7 incident triage + escalation
content.GAIGA: Global AI Governance Assembly — multilateral political body for AI treaty obligations
content.GACRLS: Global AI Compute Resource Licensing System — frontier-grade chip + cluster licensing
content.GFCO: Global Frontier-Compute Observatory — public-interest monitoring + research
content.GAID: Global AI Incident Database — public record of incidents (redacted as required)
content.GASCF: Global AI Safety Certification Framework — Cert Gold/Platinum tiers
content.GAI-COORD: Umbrella coordination body bridging ICGC, GASO, GACRA, AISI networks

content.kpis: Compute quota usage, frontier-eval pass rates, FTEWS triggers, incident counts
content.interlocks: G7 Hiroshima + Bletchley + Seoul + CoE AI Convention + FSB
content.audit: GAIVS-led independent verification + GASCF certification + GACMO market checks
content.publicTrust: GFCO + GAID public registers + public dashboards

content.computeRegistry: Quarterly attestations to GACP + GACRLS; compute receipts in WORM
content.incidentReporting: GAID + GAICS mandatory reporting within 24-72 hr depending on severity
content.evaluation: GASO-aligned evals; results in WORM + public summary
content.certification: GASCF Cert Gold by 2027, Platinum by 2029
content.interop: GACRA-conformant Annex IV + SR 11-7 + ISO 42001 evidence packs

modules.title: M7 — Enterprise AI Governance Hub + AI Safety Report Generator
modules.summary: Architecture of the Enterprise AI Governance Hub (EAGH) and AI Safety Report Generator (AISRG): UX, microservices, evidence pipeline, supervisor portal, public-facing transparency page, deterministic replay, signed PDF emission.

content.tiles: 27 board tiles incl. KPI, RCM, kill-switch SLA, evidence assembly, drift κ cosine, threat-intel
content.navigation: Org → Tribe → Track → Model → Decision; CRS-UUID drill-down to single inference
content.accessibility: WCAG 2.2 AA; lighthouse a11y ≥ 95; RTL for AR

content.ingest: Pulls from registry, evals, RAG, Sentinel, OPA decision logs, WORM
content.render: Jinja2 templates per regime (Annex IV, SR 11-7, ISO 42001, SOC 2, DPIA)
content.sign: ML-DSA-65 + RSA-PSS hybrid; PAdES PDF; in-toto attestation
content.store: S3 Object Lock + Merkle anchor + Rekor entry
content.publish: Supervisor self-serve portal + GAID public registry

content.assemble: AISRG fetches from WORM + registry + RAG lineage; assembles bundle
content.verify: Sigstore verify + Merkle proof + replay diff = 0
content.deliver: Push to supervisor portal or pull via mTLS API

content.auth: mTLS + OIDC + RBAC; per-supervisor scope (region + sector)
content.search: By model, by date, by use-case, by incident; signed export with zk-SNARK selective disclosure
content.SLA: Question intake → response ≤ 5 business days; supervisor-grade audit log
content.transparency: Public widget for zk-SNARK proof verification

content.page: Model cards, evals (redacted), incidents (per GAID), Merkle anchor proofs, kill-switch SLA history
content.replay: RPCO harness — freeze inputs, re-run, diff = 0 enforced
content.auditor-mode: Read-only auditor seat with full evidence-pack browsing + signed export
content.publicVerifier: Open-source verifier for Merkle anchors + ML-DSA + zk-SNARK proofs

modules.title: M8 — Advanced Prompt Engineering Practices (Architect-Grade)
modules.summary: Architect-grade prompt engineering: templating, variable linking, version control, testing, sharing, lineage, adversarial defence, telemetry-driven deprecation; institutional guardrails for prompts as governed artefacts.

content.engine: Jinja2 in safe sandbox; schema-aware variable types (string, number, enum, JSONSchema)
content.linking: Cross-template variable graph; auto-binding to RAG retrieval and customer context
content.constraints: Output format enforced (JSONSchema, regex, length, BNF)

content.semver: Immutable hash IDs; semver + branch + canary
content.repo: Git-backed with signed commits (ML-DSA-65) + Sigstore co-sign
content.approval: MRM + GC sign-off for Tier-1; SR 11-7 binding
content.rollback: Per-template canary + auto-rollback on κ < 0.9 or fiduciary cosine drop

content.golden: Golden-set tests; deterministic seed; replay diff = 0
content.injection: PromptArmor, Garak, internal injection corpus; Tier-1 mandatory
content.evals: Faithfulness, citation coverage ≥ 95 %, hallucination, toxicity, fairness

content.marketplace: Internal template marketplace with OPA tenant fences and GC review
content.lineage: CRS-UUID linking prompt → run → output → evidence
content.tenant: Cross-tenant sharing controlled via OPA + signed bundle
content.publishing: Public templates published with redaction review

content.telemetry: Per-template invocation count, latency, κ, cosine, faithfulness, hallucination rate
content.deprecation: Auto-deprecate templates with κ drop > 5 % or faithfulness < threshold
content.drift: Latent drift Δ ≤ 3 % per template version; alert on breach
content.audit: Per-template usage anchored daily; quarterly stewardship review

modules.title: M9 — Civilizational-Scale AI Governance Corpus
modules.summary: Civilizational-scale corpus of governance precedents, scenario analyses, treaty texts, eval methodologies, and historical incident records, with provenance + 100-year retention + redaction policy + scholarly access programme.

content.scope: Treaty texts; regulatory consultations; AISI evals; civilizational sims; incident records
content.schema: {id, source, jurisdiction, lang, date, classification, hash, signature, lineage}
content.size: Target ≥ 1 PB compressed (zstd + dictionary) over 5 years

content.provenance: in-toto SLSA L3+; per-document ML-DSA-65 signature; SBOM-style chain
content.anchoring: Daily Merkle root + Rekor + public verifier
content.redaction: GC + AI Safety Lead joint redaction; public + sealed variants
content.tamper-evidence: S3 Object Lock Compliance + cross-region replication

content.scenarios: Treaty defection, frontier-eval shortfall, FTEWS escalation, compute-registry evasion, fiduciary collapse
content.schema: World-state + actor models + capability vectors + civilizational-risk metric
content.refresh: Annual with AISI co-supervision + external assurance

content.fields: id, ts, severity (S1-S5), description (redacted), root-cause, remediation, supervisor-notified
content.replay: RPCO replay harness available for forensic studies

content.fellowships: 12 PhD + 4 postdoc per year via Sentinel Lab + university partners
content.access: Read + replay + cite; publication review by GC + AI Safety Lead
content.publications: Annual civilizational research report; public defensive disclosures

modules.title: M10 — Regulator-Ready Technical Report Sections (with <title>/<abstract>/<content> tags)
modules.summary: Twelve regulator-ready technical report sections in the machine-readable <title>/<abstract>/<content> format, each consumable by Annex IV / SR 11-7 / ISO 42001 / SOC 2 / DPIA generators; full payloads exposed via /report-sections endpoint.

content.tags: Three top-level tags; nested HTML/MD allowed inside <content>
content.encoding: UTF-8, NFC normalized, no carriage returns
content.consumers: Annex IV / SR 11-7 / AISRG / Supervisor portal

content.SOC 2 TSC: Security → R-06; Availability → R-07; Confidentiality → R-05; Processing Integrity → R-03; Privacy → R-05

content.tagged: Each section payload includes a {tagged} field with the pre-rendered <title>/<abstract>/<content> string
content.format: JSON + JSONL (one section per line) supported

modules.title: M11 — Enterprise Implementation Blueprints
modules.summary: Production blueprints for CI/CD policy gates, Kubernetes/Kafka/OPA control stacks, Terraform-deployed golden environments, Kafka ACL governance, PQC-secured WORM, zk-SNARK access control, OPA/Rego enforcement, deterministic audit replay, hyperparameter drift analysis, adversarial red teaming, Cognitive Resonance monitoring, and IR checklists.

content.ci: GitHub Actions reusable workflow; build → sign (cosign + ML-DSA) → attest (SLSA L3+ + in-toto) → conftest (Rego) → admission verify
content.golden: Terraform modules: vpc + eks + msk + s3-worm-lock + kms-pqc + iam + opa-bundle-svc + sigstore-mirror + sentinel-cluster

content.k8s: Gatekeeper + Kyverno baseline policies; OPA sidecar at p99 ≤ 8 ms; Cilium zero-egress default-deny
content.kafka: MSK 3-broker; per-topic ACLs managed via OPA; idempotent producers; WORM topic class
content.opa: Signed Rego bundles; bundle service mirrored air-gap; decision logs to Kafka WORM
content.kill-switch: Per-namespace + per-agent; 3-of-5 quorum; logical ≤ 60 s, BMC ≤ 5 min

sections.title: Kafka ACL Governance + WORM with PQC + zk-SNARK

content.acl: Per-principal per-topic READ/WRITE/ADMIN; idempotent producer required for WORM topics
content.worm: S3 Object Lock Compliance + Merkle daily anchor + ML-DSA-65 envelope per event
content.pqc-kms: FIPS 203/204 + FIPS 140-3 L4 HSM; ML-KEM-768 for envelope encryption
content.zk-snark: Groth16/PLONK proofs for selective-disclosure access; public verifier widget on supervisor portal

sections.title: OPA/Rego Enforcement, Replay, Hyperparams & Red Team

content.rego-suite: admission, egress, model-registry-required, prompt-approval, eval-pass, kill-switch quorum, GACP attestation
content.replay: RPCO: freeze inputs → re-run → diff = 0 enforced; supervisor-grade
content.hyperparams: Manifest-declared ranges; deviation = MRM + GC approval; WORM lineage
content.redteam: Garak + PromptArmor + Apollo deceptive-alignment; quarterly external; annual AISI joint

content.monitoring: Probes for Δ_drift ≤ 4 %, latent ≤ 3 %, fiduciary cosine ≥ 0.92, judge κ ≥ 0.9
content.ir-runbooks: Kill-switch invocation; WORM tamper; Sigstore compromise; RAG poisoning; prompt-injection; compute-registry evasion
content.ir-checklist: 1. Detect → 2. Triage (SLA per severity) → 3. Quorum approval → 4. Mitigate → 5. RPCO replay → 6. Evidence vault → 7. GAID notify → 8. Supervisor notify → 9. Post-incident review → 10. Remediation tracker
content.comms: Pre-drafted regulator and customer comms templates

modules.title: M12 — Tiered Enterprise Rollout Roadmaps (R1 / R2 / R3)
modules.summary: Tiered rollout roadmaps for F500 / G2000 / G-SIFI: R1 (Foundations 0-180 d), R2 (Scale 180-540 d), R3 (Civilizational Steady-State 540-1825 d); each tier specifies prerequisites, gate evidence, supervisor packs and exit criteria.

content.scope: F500 onboarding; baseline MVAGS + MGK; Tier-2 model use-cases
content.prereqs: Kill-switch quorum live; Sigstore + ML-DSA; OPA bundle service; Kafka WORM + S3 Object Lock; PQC KMS
content.deliverables: Annex IV draft for first 3 Tier-1 models; SR 11-7 packs; dashboards alpha; Prompt Architect MVP; RAG governance v1
content.exit: Gate G0 + G1 evidence packs signed; supervisor Q1 + Q2 packs delivered

content.scope: G2000 + G-SIFI; full WP-051 stack; Tier-1 model use-cases
content.prereqs: Model registry GA; EAIP draft RFC; CCaaS-PETs pilot; threat-intel dashboard; AGI sim v1
content.deliverables: All 17 critical-path items at G2/G3; supervisor self-serve portal; Cert Gold (ISO 42001) audit
content.exit: Gate G2 + G3 evidence packs signed; AISI joint exercise reports; FSB submission

content.scope: Treaty obligations; civilizational sims; ICGC + GACP attestations; Cert Platinum
content.prereqs: GACP/GACRLS/GACRA brokers; zk-SNARK verifier portal; interpretability suite; RPCO replay GA
content.deliverables: EAIP v1.0 final; CSE-X v2; civilizational research publications; MGK steady state
content.exit: Gate G4 evidence packs; Cert Platinum re-audit; public assurance program

content.R1: Quarterly Annex IV + SR 11-7 + DPIA + ISO 42001 progress
content.R2: Quarterly + ad-hoc; AISI joint reports semi-annual
content.R3: Quarterly + annual treaty + civilizational research papers
content.delivery: Supervisor self-serve portal + mTLS API + offline encrypted USB on request

content.RAG: Per track red/amber/green at each gate dry-run
content.escalation: T1-T5 escalation tiers (sprint blocker → supervisory notification)
content.PMO: Quarterly board read-out; monthly KPI tile; biweekly architecture review
content.evidence: Phase-gate Merkle bundles G0..G4 + supervisor packs anchored

modules.title: M13 — Machine-Readable Artifacts Catalogue
modules.summary: Catalogue of machine-readable artefacts produced and consumed by the master reference: JSON, JSONL, YAML, Terraform, Rego, JSONSchema, PAdES PDF, zk-SNARK proofs; URIs, signing, verification, and integration points.

content.report-sections: /api/inst-agi-master-ref-2026/report-sections → JSON array; JSONL on Accept: application/x-ndjson

content.terraform-modules: vpc, eks, msk, s3-worm-lock, kms-pqc, iam, opa-bundle-svc, sigstore-mirror, sentinel-cluster
content.yaml: K8s manifests (Gatekeeper, Kyverno, Sentinel sidecars); model manifests; OPA bundle manifests
content.schemas: JSONSchema files for EAIP envelope, model manifest, OKR rollup, gate-evidence
content.signing: Per-module ML-DSA-65 + SLSA L3+ provenance; published in Sigstore

content.library: admission, egress, model-registry-required, prompt-approval, eval-pass, kill-switch quorum, GACP attestation
content.bundles: Signed Rego bundles via bundle service; conftest in CI; OPA sidecar at runtime
content.tests: Per-policy unit tests; integration tests across simulated decisions
content.audit: Decision logs to Kafka WORM with CRS-UUID lineage

content.pdf: PAdES-signed (ML-DSA-65 + RSA-PSS hybrid); embedded JSON metadata; Annex IV / SR 11-7 / ISO 42001 / SOC 2 / DPIA
content.zk-snark: Groth16/PLONK proofs for selective disclosure (e.g. fairness pass without raw data)
content.verification: Public verifier widget + supervisor mTLS API + offline verifier binary

content.internal: PMO ingest; OKR rollup; KPI tile
content.signing-trust: Sigstore + ML-DSA-65 + Merkle anchors; public verifier

modules.title: M14 — Cross-Cutting Critical Path & Closing Checklist (2026-2030)
modules.summary: Cross-cutting critical-path summary tying together CP-01..CP-17 with phase gates G0..G4, rollout tiers R1..R3, containment tiers T0..T4 and a programme closing checklist.

content.CP-02: Sigstore + ML-DSA hybrid signing (G0; R1; DevSecOps)
content.CP-03: OPA bundle service + Rego CI (G0; R1; DevSecOps)
content.CP-04: Kafka WORM + S3 Object Lock + Merkle anchor (G0; R1; Platform)
content.CP-06: Sentinel v2.4 Cognitive Resonance probes (G1; R1; AI Research)
content.CP-07: WorkflowAI Pro agent registry (G1; R1; Platform+CAIO)
content.CP-08: Inference proxies + EAIP draft (G1; R1; Platform+Architecture)
content.CP-09: Model registry GA (G2; R2; Registry tribe)
content.CP-10: Prompt Architect templating + versioning (G1/G2; R1/R2; Prompt tribe)
content.CP-11: RAG ACL + taint + lineage (G1/G2; R1/R2; RAG tribe)
content.CP-12: Governance dashboards alpha → GA (G1/G3; R1/R2; UI tribe)
content.CP-13: Annex IV / SR 11-7 pack auto-assembly ≤ 30 min (G3; R2; Reports)
content.CP-14: AGI/ASI sim engine (CSE-X + SRASE) (G2/G3; R2/R3; Civilizational)
content.CP-16: zk-SNARK verifier + public portal (G3; R3; Security+UI)
content.CP-17: RPCO replay harness + Evidence Vault (G3; R3; Platform+MRM)

content.G0\u2192R1\u2192T0/T1: Foundations: kill-switch, signing, OPA, WORM, PQC; sandbox + pre-prod
content.G1\u2192R1\u2192T2: Alpha: Sentinel, WorkflowAI, EAIP draft, dashboards alpha; prod limited
content.G2\u2192R2\u2192T3: GA: model registry, EAIP RFC, AGI sim v1; prod full Tier-1
content.G3\u2192R2/R3\u2192T3/T4: Federation: GACP/GACRLS/GACRA, zk-SNARK, interpretability, RPCO; frontier-air-gapped
content.G4\u2192R3\u2192T4: Steady state: treaty obligations, Cert Platinum, MGK ops, civilizational research

sections.title: Programme Closing Checklist (5-Year)
content: All 17 critical-path items in steady-state operations
EAIP v1.0 published; cross-institution interop with ≥ 10 G-SIFIs
ICGC + GAID + GFCO + GAIVS + GASCF interop attested
Civilizational corpus ≥ 1 PB with daily Merkle anchors
Annual treaty + supervisor packs published with signed PDFs
Quarterly OKR rollups archived in WORM (7-year minimum)
AISI joint exercise count ≥ 20 over horizon
Public assurance programme live with public verifier + zk-SNARK widgets
Hire-plan diversity slate audits passed all years
Zero unresolved Tier-1 incidents; full RPCO replay diff = 0 on all

content.2026: G0+G1 closed; Cert Gold; EAIP RFC drafted; first AISI joint exercise
content.2027: G2 closed; model registry GA; CCaaS-PETs GA; SRASE composite ≥ 0.9 sustained
content.2028: G3 closed; EAIP v1.0; Cert Platinum; FSB submissions ratified
content.2029: MGK steady state; civilizational research papers; AISI joint count ≥ 16
content.2030: G4 close; public assurance programme; Cert Platinum re-audit pass

content.R: Chief Architect + CAIO + AI Safety Lead

code.title: OPA policy: block training-data PII without consent
K8s admission webhook for model deployment
code.snippet:
apiVersion: admissionregistration.k8s.io/v1
kind: ValidatingWebhookConfiguration
metadata:
  name: model-governance-gate
webhooks:
  - name: governance.ai.example.com
    rules:
      - apiGroups: ["ai.example.com"]
        apiVersions: ["v1"]
        resources: ["models"]
        operations: ["CREATE", "UPDATE"]

code.title: Terraform golden environment for AI workloads
code.snippet:
module "ai_golden_env" {
  source       = "./modules/ai-golden"
  region       = "eu-west-1"
  worm_bucket  = "audit-logs-pqc"
  opa_endpoint = "https://opa.governance.svc/v1/data"
  pqc_kms      = "arn:aws:kms:eu-west-1:...:key/pqc-dilithium3"
}

code.title: Kafka WORM producer with PQC signature
code.snippet:
from confluent_kafka import Producer
from pqc.sig.dilithium3 import sign
p = Producer({'bootstrap.servers': 'kafka-worm:9093', 'acks': 'all'})
evt = build_audit_event(...)
sig = sign(priv_key, canonical(evt))
p.produce('audit-worm', key=evt['eventId'], value=encode(evt, sig))
p.flush()

code.title: Deterministic replay harness
code.snippet:
def replay(run_id):
    rec = load_training_record(run_id)
    set_seed(rec['seed'])
    pin_versions(rec['artifacts'])
    out = train(rec['hyperparams'], rec['dataset_hash'])
    assert digest(out.weights) == rec['weights_hash']
    return out

code.title: CRP composite calculator
PM2 ecosystem for governance sidecar
code.snippet:
apps:
  - name: gov-sidecar
    script: ./sidecar/index.js
    env:
      OPA_URL: http://opa:8181
      WORM_TOPIC: audit-worm
      CRP_THRESHOLD: "0.90"
    instances: 2
    exec_mode: cluster

    steps:

      - run: opa test policies/ -v
      - run: conftest test manifests/ -p policies/

Tier-based model deployment guard
zk-SNARK access proof (pseudo)
Red-team prompt orchestrator
code.snippet:
for atk in load_attack_corpus('owasp-llm-top10'):
    resp = model.invoke(atk.prompt)
    score = judge(resp, atk.expected_refusal)
    log_to_worm({'attack': atk.id, 'score': score, 'resp_hash': sha(resp)})

    operations: [Read, Write]
  - principal: User:auditor
    resource: { type: topic, name: audit-worm }
    operations: [Read]

reportSections.title: Governance Framework Overview
reportSections.abstract: Summarises the nine governance pillars (P1-P9), the Minimum Governance Kernel (MGK), the Minimum Viable AGI Governance Stack (MVAGS), and the board-level RACI under which all enterprise AI is operated for the 2026-2030 horizon.
reportSections.content: The institution operates a tiered AI governance framework anchored on ISO/IEC 42001, NIST AI RMF 1.0, and the EU AI Act. Accountability is owned at Board level via the AI/Risk Committee, with the CAIO holding executive accountability and the CRO/CISO/DPO/GC providing second-line assurance. The framework defines nine pillars: P1 Accountability, P2 Transparency, P3 Fairness, P4 Privacy, P5 Security, P6 Safety, P7 Oversight, P8 Continuous Monitoring, and P9 Sustainability. MGK provides the minimum non-negotiable kernel of controls (charter, RACI, model inventory, WORM audit, OPA policy library, incident response), and MVAGS adds the AGI-specific overlay (containment tiers T0-T4, CRP composite >= 0.90, Sentinel v2.4 telemetry, MVAGS crisis sims WG-01..WG-06). All Tier-1 and Annex IV high-risk systems are subject to dual sign-off (CAIO + CRO) and quarterly Board attestation.
reportSections.tagged:
<title>Governance Framework Overview</title>
<abstract>Summarises the nine governance pillars (P1-P9), the Minimum Governance Kernel (MGK), the Minimum Viable AGI Governance Stack (MVAGS), and the board-level RACI under which all enterprise AI is operated for the 2026-2030 horizon.</abstract>
<content>The institution operates a tiered AI governance framework anchored on ISO/IEC 42001, NIST AI RMF 1.0, and the EU AI Act. Accountability is owned at Board level via the AI/Risk Committee, with the CAIO holding executive accountability and the CRO/CISO/DPO/GC providing second-line assurance. The framework defines nine pillars: P1 Accountability, P2 Transparency, P3 Fairness, P4 Privacy, P5 Security, P6 Safety, P7 Oversight, P8 Continuous Monitoring, and P9 Sustainability. MGK provides the minimum non-negotiable kernel of controls (charter, RACI, model inventory, WORM audit, OPA policy library, incident response), and MVAGS adds the AGI-specific overlay (containment tiers T0-T4, CRP composite >= 0.90, Sentinel v2.4 telemetry, MVAGS crisis sims WG-01..WG-06). All Tier-1 and Annex IV high-risk systems are subject to dual sign-off (CAIO + CRO) and quarterly Board attestation.</content>

reportSections.title: Model Inventory and Classification
reportSections.abstract: Describes the complete enterprise model inventory, the risk-tiering taxonomy (Tier-0 internal/low-risk through Tier-1 customer-facing/high-risk and Annex IV high-risk), and the lifecycle states under which each model is governed.
reportSections.content: The model inventory is maintained as a single source of truth in the AI Model Registry, schema SCH-01 (ModelCard.v2). Every model — including prompts, agents, and AI-enabled features — is registered with owner, use case, training data lineage (SCH-04), risk tier, regulatory classification (EU AI Act risk class, SR 11-7 tier, MAS FEAT category), and current lifecycle state (draft, validated, approved, deployed, monitored, retired). KPI K-02 enforces 100% coverage, audited monthly. Tier-1 models additionally carry deterministic-replay readiness (CODE-05), CRP measurement series (SCH-09), and approval-chain WORM evidence (SCH-08). Annex IV high-risk systems carry the full Annex IV technical documentation pack and pass through pre-deploy AISI joint testing.

Every model — including prompts, agents, and AI-enabled features — is registered with owner, use case, training data lineage (SCH-04), risk tier, regulatory classification (EU AI Act risk class, SR 11-7 tier, MAS FEAT category), and current lifecycle state (draft, validated, approved, deployed, monitored, retired). reportSections.title: Conceptual Soundness and Validation
reportSections.abstract: Documents the conceptual soundness, design review, and independent validation processes aligned with SR 11-7 and PRA SS1/23, including deterministic replay, hyperparameter drift control, and benchmark/eval batteries.
reportSections.content: Each model is subjected to (i) design review by 1LoD modellers using a conceptual-soundness checklist, (ii) independent validation by 2LoD MRM covering theory, implementation, outcomes, and ongoing use, and (iii) third-line internal audit on the validation process itself. Deterministic replay (CODE-05) is mandatory for all Tier-1 / Annex IV high-risk training runs, with KPI K-11 enforcing zero byte diff. Hyperparameter drift is monitored via CODE-09 with KPI K-12 set at <= 5% per dimension. Evaluation batteries (SCH-07) cover task benchmarks, fairness (4/5ths rule, K-07), robustness (adversarial perturbations), red-team OWASP LLM Top 10 (K-10), and safety evals from MVAGS. Validation reports are stored in the WORM evidence pack section 03 and refreshed annually or upon material change.

reportSections.title: Fairness, Non-discrimination, and Consumer Outcomes
reportSections.abstract: Evidences fairness, non-discrimination, and consumer-outcome controls aligned with ECOA, FCRA, FCA Consumer Duty, MAS FEAT, and EU AI Act fundamental-rights impact requirements.
reportSections.content: Fairness is governed by Pillar P3 and Risk-Control RCM-01. All decisioning models undergo disparate-impact testing using the four-fifths rule (KPI K-07 target 0.80-1.25), supplemented by equality-of-opportunity and predictive-parity metrics where decision context warrants. Adverse-action reason codes are generated through an explainability pipeline (SHAP + counterfactual, KPI K-22) and surfaced to consumers per FCRA/ECOA. FCA Consumer Duty foreseeable-harm reviews feed the Consumer Outcomes dashboard, with MAS FEAT principles evidenced via the FEAT assessment artefact (REG-07 submissions). EU AI Act Article 27 FRIA is performed for every high-risk system. Findings flow into the Risk Register (SCH-02).

reportSections.title: Privacy, Data Protection, and Cross-Border Transfers
reportSections.abstract: Sets out the privacy posture under GDPR, UK GDPR, and analogous regimes (CCPA, PDPA-SG, PDPO-HK), including lawful basis, data subject rights, PETs, and cross-border transfer instruments.
reportSections.content: Privacy is owned by the DPO under Pillar P4. Lawful basis for AI training and operation is documented per dataset (explicit consent, legitimate interest with DPIA, public task for fraud/AML). Data subject rights are operationalised through a DSAR pipeline (KPI K-08 <= 30 days), with the WORM exemption registry preserving auditability while honouring erasure obligations. Privacy-enhancing technologies (PETs) — differential privacy, k-anonymity, federated learning, secure enclaves — are deployed per data classification. Cross-border transfers use EU SCCs, UK IDTA, APAC bilateral mechanisms, and, prospectively, the ICGC data-adequacy registry. DPIAs are refreshed annually and on material change; RCM-02 documents residual risk.

reportSections.title: Security and Resilience of AI Systems
reportSections.abstract: Documents AI-specific security controls — model supply chain, prompt-injection defence, audit-log integrity, zk-SNARK access proofs, and operational resilience under PRA SS1/21 and analogous regimes.
reportSections.content: Security is governed by Pillar P5 under the CISO. The model supply chain is protected via SBOM-AI, signed model weights, and third-party assurance (KPI K-16). Prompt injection and data exfiltration are mitigated through input/output filters, Kafka ACL segregation (CODE-13), and continuous red-team exercise (CODE-12, KPI K-10). Audit-log integrity uses PQC-signed WORM (Dilithium3) with quarterly external attestation (RCM-05). Break-glass access uses zk-SNARK access proofs (CODE-11) so privileged actions are provable without exposing identity. Operational resilience tests cover Tier-1 model loss scenarios per PRA SS1/21 important business services.

reportSections.title: Safety, Containment, and AGI/ASI Frontier Controls
reportSections.abstract: Describes the AGI/ASI safety stack — Sentinel v2.4, Luminous Engine Codex, Cognitive Resonance Protocol, MVAGS crisis simulations, and containment tiers T0-T4 — and how it integrates with enterprise change control.
reportSections.content: Safety is governed by Pillar P6 under the AI Safety Lead. Sentinel v2.4 provides continuous telemetry on alignment, stability, and transparency. The Cognitive Resonance Protocol composes these into a single CRP composite (CODE-06); KPIs K-03 (>= 0.90 Tier-1) and K-04 (>= 0.95 Annex IV high-risk) are enforced in real time and surfaced to the Board AI dashboard. Containment tiers T0 (sandbox) through T4 (frontier air-gapped) gate every model deployment; transitions require WORM-logged dual sign-off (CODE-16). MVAGS crisis sims WG-01..WG-06 exercise containment, regulator notification, and shutdown procedures semi-annually. Frontier R&D additionally feeds AISI joint pre-deploy testing.

reportSections.title: Human Oversight and Accountability
reportSections.abstract: Demonstrates human-in-the-loop oversight and accountability mappings aligned with EU AI Act Article 14, GDPR Article 22, SMCR, and equivalent personal-accountability regimes.
reportSections.content: Pillar P7 (Oversight) places ultimate accountability with the Board AI/Risk Committee. SMF roles (UK SMCR) are mapped to AI accountabilities and recorded in Statements of Responsibility; equivalent registers are kept for US (Fed/OCC heightened standards), Singapore (MAS), and Hong Kong (HKMA). Every Annex IV high-risk and Tier-1 system documents the human-in-the-loop intervention points, training of overseers, and the override / pause / shutdown controls required under EU AI Act Article 14. GDPR Article 22 fully automated decisions are restricted to explicit-consent or contract-necessity bases with documented human-review escalation. Workshop W-01 ensures the Board operates at AI-literacy parity with executive layers.

reportSections.title: Continuous Monitoring, Drift, and Incident Response
reportSections.abstract: Sets out the continuous-monitoring fabric — performance, fairness, drift, CRP, security signals — and the incident-response chain coordinated by the Global AI Security Operations Centre (GAI-SOC).
reportSections.content: Pillar P8 mandates real-time monitoring across performance, fairness (K-07), data and concept drift, CRP (K-03/K-04), adversarial-robustness regression (K-21), and security telemetry. Signals flow into the governance sidecar, are evaluated against OPA policy, and trigger automated containment escalations (CODE-16) when thresholds breach. Incidents are recorded under SCH-03, with KPI K-09 (Tier-1 MTTC <= 4h) and quarterly tabletop W-06. GAI-SOC owns the playbooks for prompt-injection, data-exfiltration, model-theft, supply-chain compromise, and frontier capability-gain scenarios. Regulator notification SLAs (EU AI Act serious incident <= 15 days, others per regime) are codified and rehearsed annually (W-05).

reportSections.title: Sustainability, Energy, and Compute Footprint
reportSections.abstract: Quantifies energy and carbon footprint of AI workloads, reports against year-on-year reduction targets, and aligns with TCFD/ISSB and emerging EU AI Act sustainability disclosures.
reportSections.content: Pillar P9 (Sustainability) requires per-training-run energy (kWh) and carbon (kgCO2e) logging (SCH-06) and per-1k-inferences intensity reporting. KPI K-14 targets year-on-year energy intensity reduction of 10% and K-15 targets 15% carbon-intensity reduction. Carbon-aware scheduling routes training to low-carbon regions where SLAs allow. Annual disclosures are published under TCFD/ISSB and the AI sustainability addendum required by the GPAI code of practice. Frontier training carries a dedicated carbon budget approved at ExCo and reported to the Board.

reportSections.title: Global Governance, Treaty Alignment, and Compute Registries
reportSections.abstract: Describes the institution's alignment with the proposed 16-body global AI/compute governance architecture (ICGC, GACRA, GASO, GFMCF, GAICS, GAIVS, GACP, GATI, GACMO, FTEWS, GAI-SOC, GAIGA, GACRLS, GFCO, GAID, GASCF), and the Treaty Liaison Office responsibilities.
reportSections.content: The Treaty Liaison Office, reporting jointly to GC and CRO, owns alignment with the 16-body global architecture coordinated under the GAI-COORD umbrella. Compute clusters above the EO 14110 / GPAI thresholds are registered with the International Compute Governance Consortium (ICGC) per SCH-10 and reported quarterly to GACRA (KPIs K-13/K-20). The institution participates in GAIVS verification exercises, files material AI risk to GFMCF, and submits to GASO standards observatories. GASCF financing flows fund cross-border safety research. The Treaty Liaison Office briefs the Board annually (W-07).
reportSections.tagged:
<title>Global Governance, Treaty Alignment, and Compute Registries</title>
<abstract>Describes the institution's alignment with the proposed 16-body global AI/compute governance architecture (ICGC, GACRA, GASO, GFMCF, GAICS, GAIVS, GACP, GATI, GACMO, FTEWS, GAI-SOC, GAIGA, GACRLS, GFCO, GAID, GASCF), and the Treaty Liaison Office responsibilities.</abstract>
<content>The Treaty Liaison Office, reporting jointly to GC and CRO, owns alignment with the 16-body global architecture coordinated under the GAI-COORD umbrella. Compute clusters above the EO 14110 / GPAI thresholds are registered with the International Compute Governance Consortium (ICGC) per SCH-10 and reported quarterly to GACRA (KPIs K-13/K-20). The institution participates in GAIVS verification exercises, files material AI risk to GFMCF, and submits to GASO standards observatories. GASCF financing flows fund cross-border safety research. The Treaty Liaison Office briefs the Board annually (W-07).</content>

reportSections.title: Public Transparency and Assurance
reportSections.abstract: Documents the public assurance programme — model cards, GPAI summaries, third-party assurance reports, and the public verifier service — that closes the trust loop with consumers, civil society, and regulators.
reportSections.content: Public assurance is delivered through (i) consumer-facing model cards summarising purpose, limitations, and recourse; (ii) GPAI training-data summaries per EU AI Act Article 53; (iii) annual AI Transparency Report aggregating KPIs K-01..K-24 and material incidents; (iv) third-party assurance reports (ISAE 3000 or equivalent) on the governance system; and (v) a public verifier service exposing zk-SNARK proofs over the WORM Merkle root, allowing external parties to verify audit-log integrity without accessing underlying data. The 2030 target is full public assurance programme operation with re-audited ISO 42001 Platinum certification and regulator endorsement of the AISRG R-01..R-12 format as the institution's standard regulator submission.
